name: My lawyer cited fake AI cases in court and got sanctioned in NY, what happens now?
description: Lawyers face $5K fines, judge notifications, bar review; verify all AI output before filing.[1]

mainEntity.name: My lawyer cited fake AI cases in court and got sanctioned in NY, what happens now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Lawyers face $5K fines, judge notifications, bar review; verify all AI output before filing.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: The lawyers and firm face monetary fines, court orders to notify judges, and potential bar discipline. In the Levidow case, Judge Castel imposed a $5,000 fine and required letters to affected judges for citing non-existent ChatGPT-generated cases.[1] Bad faith Rule 11 violations led to sanctions as they failed to verify citations. Key factors: failure to check AI output, ignoring judicial scrutiny, firm liability. Immediately consult bar counsel, disclose to clients, implement AI verification protocols; retain independent counsel for defense. These precedents heighten scrutiny on AI use in NY federal courts.
